reason: ROM is the standard acronym for Read-Only Memory; RAM is the Random Access one.
Figure shows all eight K-map cells (minterms 0-7) filled with 1, so the function is the constant 1.
xy + xy' = x(y + y') = x·1 = x.
Round Robin is FCFS/FIFO with a time quantum added, i.e. the preemptive form of FIFO.
Balanced means subtree HEIGHTS differ by at most 1, not equal node counts; false.
The four maintenance types are corrective, adaptive, perfective, preventive; 'Customer Serviced' is not one.
A bridge is a data-link device that joins two LANs/LAN segments and filters frames by MAC address.
Repeaters only regenerate the electrical/optical signal, so they operate at the physical layer.
Regenerating a signal with no filtering is exactly a repeater; a bridge filters by MAC.
Oracle is the archetypal commercial DBMS, though Access and SQL Server are also commercial DBMSs - question has multiple defensible answers; only Linux is clearly wrong.
OSI reference model has 7 layers (physical through application).
Most common textbook count is 6 (planning, analysis, design, implementation, testing, maintenance), but 5/7-phase variants are equally standard - question is convention-dependent.
Verifying results against the software specification is the testing/verification phase.
DNS = Domain Name System.
DHCP = Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol.
IPv4 addresses are 32 bits (four octets).
IPv6 addresses are 128 bits (eight 16-bit groups).

blindAnswer: T=0 -> Q(t+1)=Q(t) (hold); T=1 -> Q(t+1)=Q(t)' (toggle); characteristic eq Q(t+1) = T XOR Q(t) = T·Q(t)' + T'·Q(t)
reason: Standard T flip-flop: toggle on T=1, hold on T=0.

blindAnswer: J=0,K=0 -> hold Q(t); J=0,K=1 -> reset to 0; J=1,K=0 -> set to 1; J=1,K=1 -> toggle Q(t)'; characteristic eq Q(t+1) = J·Q(t)' + K'·Q(t)
reason: Standard JK flip-flop behaviour and characteristic equation.
Tie J and K together and drive both from T; J=K=T gives hold on 0 and toggle on 1, so no extra gate is needed.
